skills.description: Generates detailed conversational post-mortem breakdowns of completed tasks to accelerate learning and reveal the underlying decision-making process.
trigger_conditions: Automatic execution upon completion of any task or project
Revealing the underlying decision-making process of a completed task
Extracting transferrable lessons and identifying pitfalls for future reference

skills.description: A skill that generates detailed, chronological, and time-stamped notes from video content or transcripts, capturing all technical concepts, examples, visuals, and synthesizing them into a structured format.
anti_triggers: Summarizing content without capturing all details
Omitting any part of the input

skills.description: A documentation architect that produces structured wiki catalogues and onboarding guides from codebases.
trigger_conditions: User asks to create a wiki
User wants to understand project structure or architecture
User asks for a table of contents or documentation plan
User asks for an onboarding guide or zero to hero path
anti_triggers: Small repos (≤10 files): Getting Started only

skills.description: Generate structured changelogs from git history for users asking about recent changes, changelog summaries, or wanting to understand development activity.
trigger_conditions: User wants to understand recent development activity

skills.description: Generate two complementary onboarding documents that together give any engineer — from newcomer to principal — a complete understanding of a codebase.
trigger_conditions: User asks for onboarding docs or getting-started guides
User wants to help new team members understand a codebase
anti_triggers: User is already an expert in the codebase

skills.description: Generates comprehensive technical documentation pages with evidence-based depth, following a structured procedure and mandatory requirements.
trigger_conditions: User asks to document a specific component
User wants a technical deep-dive with diagrams
A wiki catalogue section needs its content generated
anti_triggers: Guessing code paths from file names instead of reading implementation

skills.description: Architectural investigator that scans a repository to map the high-level architecture, identify the critical path, surface integrations, and flag redundant code.
trigger_conditions: User asks to analyze, investigate, scan, or explain the current workspace or a specific repository.
User has just fetched or cloned a repository and wants to understand it.
User asks for a high-level overview of architecture, business logic, or directory structure.
User asks to identify technical debt, redundant code, or core dependencies within the current project.
anti_triggers: User explicitly requests modifications or changes to the codebase during analysis.
